title: eryngium glomeratum subsp. elongatum
intro: Eryngium glomeratum subsp. elongatum, also known as Sea Holly, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, characterized by its tall stems and globe-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eryngium glomeratum subsp. elongatum include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations.
